person.name: Sir Adam Hepburn Master of Hailes, sheriff Berkwickshire

nameForms.fullText: Sir Adam Hepburn Master of Hailes, sheriff Berkwickshire

person.alternateName: Sheriff of Berwickshire Master of Hailes

birthPlace.address: Hailes Castle, Prestonkirk, East Lothian, Scotland

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Adam Hepburn, Master of Hailes (after 1432-1479) was Sheriff of Berwickshire in April 1467, and had a charter of confirmation of Dunsyre in the sheriffdom of Lanarkshire, dated 13 October 1475, being thereafter designated 'of Dunsyre'.

Family
Adam Hepburn, Master of Hailes, was the son of Sir Patrick Hepburn, 1st Lord Hailes and Ellen Wallace. He was not of age to marry on 2 February 1448, the date of the marriage contract agreed between his father, and Alexander Home, 1st Lord Home, which settled the contract of marriage between Adam and Helen Home, Sir Alexander's daughter by his spouse Marion Lauder.

«b»Children«/b»
1.) Patrick Hepburn, 1st Earl of Bothwell (c. 1452-18 October 1508).

2.) George Hepburn (c. 1454-9 September 1513)

3.) Margaret Hepburn (c. 1456-8 November 1542 married Sir David Wemyss of Wemyss and (bef 4 Dec 1488) Henry Sinclair, 4th Lord Sinclair.

4.) Adam Hepburn (c. 1457-9 September 1513)

5.) Elizabeth (Agnes) Hepburn (born c. 1461) married Alexander Home

«b»Intrigue«/b»
Hepburn is believed to have intrigued with the widowed Queen Mary of Gueldres, a young and beautiful woman. He attached himself to the party of the Boyds, and was concerned in the seizure of King James III at Linlithgow on 9 July 1466, for which he obtained a remission from Parliament dated 13 October that year.

Adam Hepburn of Dunsyre is one of the several illustrious jurors on an Assize, 5 March 1470/1, which acquitted Andrew Ker of Cessford of aiding and abetting James Douglas, 3rd Earl of Angus "traitor from England within Scotland", for his association with Robert Boyd, 1st Lord Boyd after he was declared a rebel, and other accusations, all of which Ker had denied. Others on the jury were Archibald Douglas, 5th Earl of Angus, David Lindsay, 3rd Earl of Crawford, Alexander Cunningham, Lord Kilmaurs, James Hamilton, 1st Lord Hamilton, and Sir Alexander Lauder of Haltoun.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Adam Hepburn Master of Hailes, sheriff Berkwickshire於1435年1月出生在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安普雷斯頓吉可時，他的父親Patrick Hepburn 1st Lord Hailes24歲，母親Ellen Wallace of Auchinbothy21歲。他和Lady Helen Home至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1479年12月在英國蘇格蘭藍納可爾波維爾享年44歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭費伏考登亞斯。

nameMeanings.name: Hepburn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Durham) and Scottish: habitational name from Hepburn in Chillingham (Northumberland), Hebron (Northumberland), or Hebburn in Jarrow (Durham). The placenames probably all derive from Old English hēah ‘high’ + byrgen ‘burial place, tumulus’. Compare Hebron .

variants.name: Eborn
meanings: English (Middlesex, Buckinghamshire, and Oxfordshire): variant of Eburne, itself a possible variant of Hepburn . However, the placename Hepburn comes from northeastern England, and the H- spellings are a late development, which reinforces the suspicion that the name may have a different but unknown derivation.

variants.name: Hebron
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Hebron (Northumberland), possibly from Old English hēah ‘high’ + byrgen ‘burial place, tumulus’. See also Hepburn .
Czech: from the Biblical placename (II Samuel 2: 1-3), said to mean ‘alliance’ in Hebrew.

variants.name: Heyburn
meanings: English: habitational name from a lost or unidentified place, probably in southern England, named with an unknown first element + Old English burna ‘spring, stream’.
Irish (Ulster): possibly a variant of Scottish   Hepburn  .

nameMeanings.name: Adam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Adam Hepburn Master of Hailes, sheriff Berkwickshire was born in January 1435, in Prestonkirk, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Patrick Hepburn 1st Lord Hailes, was 24 and his mother, Ellen Wallace of Auchinbothy, was 21. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Lady Helen Home. He died in December 1479, in Bothwell, Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 44, and was buried in Cowdenbeath, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Adam Hepburn Master of Hailes, sheriff Berkwickshire est né en janvier 1435, à Prestonkirk, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Patrick Hepburn 1st Lord Hailes, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Ellen Wallace of Auchinbothy, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Lady Helen Home. Il est décédé en décembre 1479, à Bothwell, Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Cowdenbeath, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

Er hatte mit Lady Helen Home mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Adam Hepburn Master of Hailes, sheriff Berkwickshireが1435年1月にイギリススコットランドエアスト ロスアンプレストンキルクで生まれた時，父Patrick Hepburn 1st Lord Hailesは24歳，母Ellen Wallace of Auchinbothyは21歳であった。彼はLady Helen Homeとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1479年12月にイギリススコットランドラナルクシュアイアボスウエルで44歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドフィフコウデンベアスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Adam Hepburn Master of Hailes, sheriff Berkwickshire(이)가 1435년 1월 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 프레스토커크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Patrick Hepburn 1st Lord Hailes(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Ellen Wallace of Auchinbothy(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Lady Helen Home(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1479년 12월 영국 스코틀랜드 랜아르크실르 벗h웰에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 코우덴브이스에 묻혔다.

Он умер декабрь 1479 г. в местности ботвелл, ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности коуденбит, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания.
